channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - wccftech.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for wccftech.com

item.title: Crimson Desert: Complete Walkthrough and Guides Hub
item.content:encoded:
Crimson Desert is Pearl Abyss’s ambitious open-world epic, taking players to the war-torn continent of Pywel. Following Kliff, the leader of the Greymane mercenaries, you will navigate political intrigue, master brutal combat, and explore a world filled with mythical creatures and ancient mysteries to restore the mercenary company after it was decimated by a surprise attack from the rival Black Bears.

In this Complete Walkthrough and Guides Hub, you will find everything you need to rebuild the Greymanes, master the complex combat system, and uncover the secrets of the Abyss.

Table of contents

  • Getting Started
  • Combat Mechanics
  • Character Progression and Skills
  • World Exploration
    • Challenges
  • Boss Strategies
  • Troubleshooting
  • Review

Getting Started

Pywel is a vast and unforgiving land where you are mostly on your own to figure out the game's intricacies. These guides will help you get started, and make you enjoy the game from the very beginning.

  • How to Expand Inventory Space
  • How to Change Time of Day
  • How to Earn Money Fast

Combat Mechanics

Combat in Crimson Desert blends traditional swordplay with wrestling-style grapples and elemental magic for an intricate yet explosive combination.

  • Best Early Weapon: How to Use the Broken Spear and Its Overpowered Evasive Slash Counter
  • Use This Broken Skill To Wipe Out Large Groups Of Enemies

Character Progression and Skills

Crimson Desert doesn't feature a traditional XP system, as characters are enhanced with items obtained through exploration and discovery.

  • Best Skills to Learn First For Kliff
  • Best Stats to Level Up First
  • How to Respec and Reset Skill Points
  • How to Summon Companions and Swap Characters

World Exploration

The continent of Pywel is divided into five massive, seamless regions, filled with all sorts of secrets to discover, puzzles to solve and challenges to complete

  • How to Unlock Fast Travel and Find Abyss Nexuses
  • Fast Travel Trick: How to Teleport Anywhere Using Character Swapping

Challenges

  • How to Clear the Sliding Down the Slope Challenge (Coming soon!)

Boss Strategies

Crimson Desert features over 70 unique bosses that will put your skills to the test with varied movesets that can make short work of your characters.

  • How to Beat the Marni's Excavatron (Coming soon!)
  • How to Beat the Reed Devil (Coming soon!)

Crafting & Management

Crimson Desert features a crafting system that allows you to create and enhance your own gear, as well as deep management of the Greymanes camp.

In-depth guides on crafting and camp management will go live soon

Troubleshooting

Although Crimson Desert has few bugs and issues, some may still creep up in some versions of the game.

  • How to Fix DualSense Controller Icons On PC (Bluetooth Fix)

Review

Is Crimson Desert a game worth your time? Check out my review to learn why the game is, in my opinion, among the best open-world games of the current generation.

All guides are currently based on a 30+ hours playthrough in the PC version of the game. Screenshots captured from the same version.

Last updated: March 19, 2026

Follow Wccftech on Google to get more of our news coverage in your feeds.



https://wccftech.com/how-to/crimson-desert-complete-walkthrough-and-guides-hub/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Crimson Desert Combat Too Hard? Use This Broken Skill to Wipe Out Large Groups Of Enemies
item.content:encoded:
If you find yourself constantly overwhelmed by enemies in Crimson Desert, you aren’t alone. The early game, in particular, is extremely difficult, so much so that the huge enemy numbers Kliff has to fight as he attempts to restore the Greymanes can make the entire experience very frustrating

While the game asks a lot out of you at the beginning of the game, there is a very simple yet effective way to even the odds against this huge enemy numbers: the Blinding Flash Finisher skill.

How to Unlock the Blinding Flash Finisher

Unlocking Blinding Flash Finisher is really easy, and can be done as soon as you learn Blinding Flash in the early game.

Requirements:

  1. Blinding Flash Lv. 1.
  2. 1 Abyss Artifact

The Crowd Eraser

Reminiscent of the Ultimate Technique from the Ninja Gaiden series, the Blinding Flash Finisher is one of the best tools for managing crowds. It is so dominant that it continues to be a great option for a long time, even after you have learned other powerful skills.

To use Blinding Flash Finisher, you have to:

  • Hold L1 + R1 (PlayStation) or LB + RB (Xbox) to activate Blade Flash
  • Use the left stick to blind as many enemies as possible
  • Press R2 (PlayStation) or RT (Xbox) multiple times to unleash a series of quick attacks against all the blinded enemies

There are a few reasons why Blinding Flash Finisher is so good, it is effectively broken against crowds.

  • When using the skill, the world slows down, essentially preventing enemies from damaging Kliff
  • Kliff automatically dashes from one enemy to the next as long as he has Spirit to unleash the final attack.
  • Blinding Flash Finisher's final slash unleashes a massive shockwave that knocks back any survivors, preventing you from being assaulted immediately once the animation ends.

In addition to the above, it is very easy to spam Blinding Flash Finisher at almost all times. As damage is so high, you will likely kill several enemies during the flurry, and, as attacks and kills restore Spirit, the cost of using the skill is almost nullified.

In my playthrough, I struggled consistently in the early game against the armies of enemies pouring out of occupied territories, and even bringing another character into the fray didn't help much. Eventually, I stopped fighting fair, as the AI certainly does. After unlocking the Blinding Flash Finisher and combining it with the Spear's Evasive Slash Counter Stance, I stopped struggling.

Try this skill out at least once and you will see: it instantly makes combat more enjoyable, giving you the breathing room needed to experiment with every other skill in Kliff's arsenal

This concludes our guide. For more help, visit our Crimson Desert Complete Walkthrough and Guides Hub.

Guide based on a 30+ hours playthrough in the PC version of the game. Screenshots captured from the same version.

Follow Wccftech on Google to get more of our news coverage in your feeds.



https://wccftech.com/how-to/crimson-desert-combat-too-hard-use-this-broken-skill-to-wipe-out-large-groups-of-enemies/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Crimson Desert – How to Change Time of Day
item.content:encoded:
In Crimson Desert, changing the time of day is as important as it is in every open-world game, if not even more. With some bounty targets only being out and about only at certain hours, and with nights being dark enough to impede your visibility, there are times when you may want to move time forward.

Table of contents

  • How to Change Time of Day
  • Can You Always Change the Time of Day?

How to Change Time of Day

Unlike the vast majority of open-world games, you cannot change the time of day in Crimson Desert by picking an option from the main menu.

To advance time, you must locate a Bonfire anywhere in the world and do the following:

  1. Approach the fire.
  2. Press L1 (PS5) or LB (Xbox) to focus on the fire.
  3. Move your cursor to the bonfire icon and press X (PS5) or A (Xbox) to access the "Wait" menu.
  4. Choose the duration. You can choose to advance time by 3, 6, or 12 hours.

While finding a bonfire every time you want to advance time can be annoying, the game keeps track of all your discovered bonfires on the map. They are identified by a small fire icon.

Alternatively, you can also use beds to rest and thus change the time of day.

Can You Always Change the Time of Day?

Unlike many other open-world games, you cannot spam the Wait mechanics to skip days at a time in Crimson Desert. The game features a rest cooldown, which makes it so your character needs to actually be fatigued from travel, combat, or the passage of time before they can rest again at a bonfire. If the option is greyed out, you'll need to head back out into Pywel for a while before the next nap.

This concludes our guide. For more help, visit our Crimson Desert Complete Walkthrough and Guides Hub.

Guide based on a 30+ hours playthrough in the PC version of the game. Screenshots captured from the same version.

Follow Wccftech on Google to get more of our news coverage in your feeds.



https://wccftech.com/how-to/crimson-desert-how-to-change-time-of-day/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Crimson Desert – Best Stats to Level Up First
item.content:encoded:
In Crimson Desert, Kliff’s physical growth is dictated by three core attributes: Health, Spirit, and Stamina. As you increase these stats by using Abyss Artifacts, which cannot be refunded without another rare type of Abyss Artifact, rather than traditional XP grinding, how you level up certain stats will determine how well you will do as you take your first steps in Pywell to restore the Greymanes.

Table of contents

  • All Upgradable Stats
  • Stats Leveling Up Priority Ranking
    • 1. Stamina
    • 2. Health
    • 3. Spirit

All Upgradable Stats

All playable characters in Crimson Desert have three stats that can be upgraded via the Skill Tree using Abyss Artifacts.

  • Health: Your total hit points. This determines how much damage you can take before being defeated.
  • Spirit: This resource is consumed by certain skills, such as Palm Strike. It is primarily replenished by attacking enemies, but also with items.
  • Stamina: The most versatile stat. It is consumed by sprinting, climbing, heavy combat strings (R2/RT) and most other melee skills. It also dictates how much damage you can block.

Stats Leveling Up Priority Ranking

1. Stamina

Without a doubt, Stamina is the most important stat to prioritize early on for the following reasons.

  • Exploration is the heart of the Crimson Desert experience. Higher stamina allows you to climb for longer, and generally traverse the map with fewer interruptions.
  • Because you are often pitted against massive groups of enemies, blocking is essentia. A small Stamina bar will see your guard broken often, leaving you with no stamina for a short time to perfom anything besides your basic light attack string.
  • Many powerful skills, including a broken Spear skill that can make short of work of mobs and bosses, consume stamina. Advanced abilities, such as Aerial Swing (Axiom Force Level 3), require a high total maximum Stamina to even activate as well.

2. Health

While Stamina is crucial to enhance early on for its traversal and combat applications, Health is a more straightforward stat.

  • Given the aggressive nature of enemies and the considerable power of bosses, having more Health is necessary to survive without heavily relying on healing items. However, it takes second place in this priority ranking because most early-game damage can be mitigated through movement and blocking, which becomes more effective with higher Stamina.

3. Spirit

Spirit is the least vital stat to level up for the early game.

  • As Spirit is restored simply by attacking, you will rarely find yourself out of it if you don't abuse certain abilities. The starting pool is generally enough to handle the starting skills without needing a permanent boost.

NOTE: It is important to note that any stat improvement made for Kliff is also applied to the other playable characters. As such, you do not need to worry about Damiane and Oonkga remaining behind.

This concludes our guide. For more help, visit our Crimson Desert Complete Walkthrough and Guides Hub.

Guide based on a 30+ hours playthrough in the PC version of the game. Screenshots captured from the same version.

Follow Wccftech on Google to get more of our news coverage in your feeds.



https://wccftech.com/how-to/crimson-desert-best-stats-to-level-up-first/
